channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Why Melbourne’s preference for black clothing reflects its climate and cultural identity
item.description: The article explores why Melbourne has developed a strong cultural association with wearing black clothing, arguing that the trend is shaped by a combination of environmental, historical, and cultural...

item.title: Verulam shooting linked to ongoing gang turf war following death of Anthony Gounden
item.description: Violence has escalated in Verulam following the assassination of alleged gang figure Anthony 'Tatu' Gounden, with authorities and private security reporting further deadly incidents linked to an ongoi...

item.title: Kingston childcare centre destroyed in early morning fire, police investigate
item.description: A major fire destroyed the Treehouse Early Learning Centre on Juers Street, Kingston, south of Brisbane, in the early hours of Monday morning. At least six fire crews were called to the scene around 1...

item.title: Centre launches Khet Bachao Abhiyan to brace for weak monsoon and promote balanced input use
item.description: The Indian government is preparing for a challenging sowing season due to forecasts of a weak monsoon and rising costs of inputs like fertilizers and diesel. To mitigate potential agricultural losses,...

item.title: Experts Investigate Suspected Ebola Cases in Brazil Amid Central African Outbreak
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in central Africa has claimed around 250 lives and is spreading more rapidly than aid organisations can respond. The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has reported over 1,000 c...

item.title: Severe winter weather warning issued for South Africa with snow, heavy rain and rough seas expected
item.description: The South African Weather Service (SAWS) has issued warnings for a significant winter weather system expected to affect large parts of the country during the first week of June. Conditions are forecas...

item.title: Sandiganbayan to Review Plunder Case Against Senator Jinggoy Estrada
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada’s legal situation is currently in the hands of the Sandiganbayan Fifth Division, which will decide whether to give due course to his motions seeking judicial determination of p...

item.title: New York embraces underdog spirit through Knicks finals run and mayor Zohran Mamdani
item.description: New York City is experiencing a surge of optimism driven by the New York Knicks’ unexpected run to the NBA finals and the public profile of its young mayor, Zohran Mamdani. The city has been marked by...

item.title: Cowry Securities ranks second in NGX weekly broker performance report with ₦18.19bn transactions
item.description: The Nigerian Exchange (NGX) has released its weekly broker performance report for the period of May 25th to May 29th, 2026, and Cowry Securities Limited came out as one of the top-performing dealing m...

item.title: Complete list of songs featured in the ‘Deli Boys’ series
item.description: ‘Deli Boys’ is a comedy series created by Abdullah Saeed, following Pakistani-American brothers Mir and Raj as they unexpectedly inherit their late father's secret drug empire. The series debuted in M...

item.title: Quebec national library launches experimental AI cultural databank to strengthen representation of local and Indigenous content
item.description: Quebec’s national library and archives institution, Bibliothèque et Archives nationales du Québec (BAnQ), has begun the experimental phase of an ambitious project to build a cultural and government da...

item.title: Vancouver parks propose designated smoking zones for select public events
item.description: The City of Vancouver has recommended establishing designated smoking areas at several upcoming public events held in city parks. This measure aims to balance the enjoyment of public spaces with healt...

item.title: Israeli forces capture Beaufort Castle ridge amid renewed fighting in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli troops have taken control of the historic Beaufort Castle and its surrounding ridge in southern Lebanon following heavy clashes with Hezbollah, according to the Israeli military. The 900-year-...

item.title: Typhoon Domeng exits PAR but continues to intensify southwest monsoon rains in parts of Luzon and Visayas
item.description: Typhoon Domeng (international name Jangmi) is expected to exit the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) on Monday, June 1, 2026, either in the morning or afternoon. Although the storm is moving awa...

item.title: Ontario man facing new stunt driving charges while awaiting trial in 2024 fatal crash case
item.description: An Ontario man who is already awaiting trial in connection with a fatal collision in Brampton is now facing additional charges related to alleged stunt driving. The 20-year-old, from Woodstock, Ontari...

item.title: Nicaraguan Indigenous Leader Brooklyn Rivera Dies in Custody After 2023 Arrest
item.description: Brooklyn Rivera, a prominent Indigenous leader from Nicaragua and longtime advocate for the rights of the Miskito people, has died after spending nearly three years in government custody. Rivera was a...

item.title: Mkhwebane requests US investigation into Phala Phala cash allegations linked to President Ramaphosa
item.description: Former Public Protector Busisiwe Mkhwebane, through her foundation, has formally requested that United States law enforcement authorities, including the FBI and the Department of Justice, investigate ...

item.title: Rian Fernandez explains design inspiration for Gazini Ganados' evening gown at MGI All Stars
item.description: The article focuses on Filipino designer Rian Fernandez as he discusses the creative inspiration behind the evening gown worn by beauty queen Gazini Ganados during the MGI All Stars event. Published b...

item.title: Khana All Stars to Host Akpajo All Stars in Return Friendly Match in Bori
item.description: Khana All Stars FC in Khana Local Government Area, Rivers State, are set to host Akpajo All Stars in a return leg friendly football match scheduled for tomorrow at the Ken Saro-Wiwa Polytechnic footba...

item.title: Nine arrested after disorder at north London football parade following Champions League final defeat
item.description: Nine people were arrested during a large-scale football parade in north London that descended into disorder following Arsenal’s Champions League final defeat to Paris Saint-Germain. The Metropolitan P...
